inclusion_criteria: Human observational studies (cross-sectional, case–control, or cohort) that include individuals with bipolar disorder diagnosed via DSM/ICD criteria or documented clinical records
Include at least one comparator group: healthy controls and/or individuals with unipolar depression or schizophrenia
Report outcomes on basal free intracellular calcium concentration ([Ca2+]i) in platelets or lymphocytes and/or [Ca2+]i responses to stimulation with serotonin (5-HT) or thrombin
Measurements performed ex vivo in peripheral blood cells with extractable group-level data (e.g., means/SD, medians/IQR, or data enabling extraction)
exclusion_criteria: Non-human studies, cell lines, or post-mortem/brain tissue studies; studies measuring calcium only in non-platelet/lymphocyte cells (e.g., neurons, fibroblasts)
Studies without a bipolar disorder group or where BD data cannot be separated; or without an appropriate comparator (no healthy control or psychiatric comparator)
Studies that do not measure intracellular [Ca2+]i in platelets/lymphocytes or do not assess 5-HT- or thrombin-stimulated [Ca2+]i responses
Interventional drug trials reporting only acute pharmacologic effects without baseline/comparator data, and non-primary research (reviews, editorials, conference abstracts without extractable data)
screening_criteria.description: Select human observational studies comparing individuals with bipolar disorder to healthy controls and/or unipolar depression or schizophrenia on basal and stimulated intracellular calcium in platelets or lymphocytes; exclude non-relevant tissues, non-human work, absent comparators, irrelevant outcomes, and non-primary reports.

included_articles.title: Intracellular calcium signalling in peripheral cells of patients with bipolar affective disorder.
Elevated basal and thapsigargin-stimulated intracellular calcium of platelets and lymphocytes from bipolar affective disorder patients measured by a fluorometric microassay.
Abnormal intracellular calcium ion concentration in platelets and lymphocytes of bipolar patients.
Enhanced calcium response to serotonin in platelets from patients with affective disorders.
Altered 5-HT-induced calcium response in the presence of staurosporine in blood platelets from bipolar disorder patients.
Elevated platelet intracellular calcium concentration in bipolar depression.
Resting and thrombin-stimulated cytosolic calcium in platelets of patients with alcoholic withdrawal, bipolar manic disorder and chronic schizophrenia.
Serotonin-induced platelet intracellular calcium mobilization in depressed patients.
Increased platelet intracellular calcium ion concentration is specific to bipolar disorder.
Serotonin-induced platelet intracellular calcium mobilization in various psychiatric disorders: is it specific to bipolar disorder?
[The mechanism of enhanced platelet intracellular calcium mobilization stimulated by serotonin--in the pathophysiology of mood disorders].
